channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: onLygal incorpora a Rubén Gómez como delegado territorial para la zona Centro
item.description: onLygal ha anunciado el nombramiento de Rubén Gómez Gismera como delegado territorial para la zona Centro, asumiendo también la responsabilidad de alianzas estratégicas en el Canal Mediado . En este sentido, reportará a Ramón Gómez, director comercial del Canal Mediado. Licenciado en Economía y en Ciencias Actuariales y Financieras por la Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, con postgrado en Ahorro y Previsión por la UPF Barcelona School of Management y formación como corredor de seguros, aporta más de 20 años de experiencia en el sector asegurador , con una trayectoria tanto en gestión técnica como en dirección comercial y desarrollo de alianzas con aseguradoras y empresas tecnológicas.

item.title: Guillermo Hoces se une a Munich Re Specialty como country claims manager
item.description: Como anunció ayer a través de su perfil de LinkedIn Gianluca Piscopo, managing director, la oficina de Munich Re Specialty Madrid ha reforzado su equipo de Reclamaciones con Guillermo Hoces Hurtado como country claims manager. Procede de Everest , donde llevaba algo más de dos años y donde hasta ahora ocupaba el cargo de deputy head of claims Continental Europe. Licenciado en Derecho y Economía, lleva más de 15 años de trayectoria profesional en el sector , en el que también ha trabajado en Axa XL, XL Catlin y Asemas.

item.title: Allianz y WWF invertirán 270.000 euros en restaurar el río Mijares tras la DANA
item.description: Allianz y WWF han puesto en marcha un proyecto de restauración ecológica en el entorno del Río Mijares, entre los municipios valencianos de Buñol y Yátova, con el objetivo de recuperar los ecosistemas afectados por la DANA de octubre de 2024 y reforzar la resiliencia del territorio frente a futuros episodios climáticos extremos. La iniciativa contará con una inversión de 270.000 euros durante los próximos tres años y contempla actuaciones sobre el cauce y la vegetación de ribera para mejorar la capacidad natural del río de absorber crecidas y reducir el impacto de posibles inundaciones. Entre las medidas previstas figuran la eliminación de especies invasoras, la limpieza selectiva del cauce, la instalación de estructuras naturales para reducir la fuerza del agua y la reforestación con especies autóctonas.

item.title: State Regulatory Surge, Federal Shifts Reshaping Workers’ Comp
item.description: Workers’ compensation is entering a period of heightened complexity as regulatory changes and policy shifts continue to unfold across federal and state levels. Although workers’ comp is governed at the state level, federal developments continue to influence the broader property/casualty landscape in meaningful ways. So far this year, a surge in legislative activity, combined with evolving federal healthcare policy and persistent medical cost pressures, is reshaping the operating environment.

item.title: Insured losses from late-November storms in Australia trimmed: Perils
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Michael Hannigan returns to AIG in Australia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Allstate returns some Sanders 2022-1 aggregate cat bond principal to investors, extends rest
item.description: Allstate has returned some principal from one of its outstanding aggregate catastrophe bonds to investors, while extending the maturity on the remaining amount, having ended the last annual aggregate risk period that its cat bonds were subject to with total pre-tax reported catastrophe losses just shy of $4 billion. We don’t know what the qualifying loss accumulation was against these Sanders Re III 2022-1 Class C notes, but we’ve now learned that at the end of the annual risk period some of their principal was returned to investors, with the remainder now having an extended maturity date. Recall that Allstate’s annual aggregate risk period for its nationwide (ex-Florida) catastrophe bond backed reinsurance protection runs from April 1st each year.

item.title: The return of “Humpty Dumpty” as Florida reinsurance conditions ease?
item.description: As reinsurance market conditions ease meaningfully for Florida based insurers at the mid-year renewals, an analyst report has cited American Integrity CEO Bob Ritchie as saying the easing is around more than just price, with improvements in terms and conditions suggesting “Humpty Dumpty is getting put back together.” TD Cowen analysts met with a number of insurance market participants in the Tampa Bay area recently, concluding that the Florida market is loosening at primary and reinsurance levels, while hearing of irrational behaviour from some newer market participants. Reinsurance market dynamics have always been a driver for competitive tendencies in Florida’s insurance market and with more pronounced price softening being seen at these reinsurance renewals, with rates said down 15% +, it’s leading to weaker terms and conditions, the analysts said. American Integrity Insurance CEO Bob Ritchie told the analysts that “Humpty Dumpty is getting put back together,” as reinsurance coverage options improve for protection buyers.

item.title: Addresscloud adds Skyblu’s high-resolution European wildfire model
item.description: Addresscloud, a UK geocoding and property intelligence expert, has adopted the high-resolution, pan-European wildfire risk model developed by Skyblu, an AI solutions provider. Skyblu’s high-resolution new model delivers 20-metre resolution risk scoring across Europe, offering significantly greater granularity than traditional datasets. The pair said that this partnership reflects growing demand for high-resolution climate intelligence in underwriting, with both companies already supporting wind and hail risk modelling in selected markets such as France and Spain, and planning a broader European rollout.

item.title: Weak cyber hygiene among smaller businesses exposes protection gap: KYND
item.description: Smaller businesses exhibit widespread weaknesses in cyber hygiene, including a lack of basic email security protections, highlighting cyber exposure and a significant opportunity for insurers to support improved resilience, according to Ben Duffy, VP and Head of North America at KYND. Cyber risk intelligence provider KYND has released research based on 7,980 small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) across the US and Canada, and 830 UK small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). Despite this, cyber insurance penetration among SMEs and SMBs remains relatively low — often estimated at below 10% in many segments — highlighting a substantial gap between cyber exposure and protection.

item.title: SiriusPoint names Paul Beattie and Ed Winter as Heads of new Crisis Solutions platform
item.description: SiriusPoint, a specialty insurer and reinsurer, has appointed Paul Beattie and Ed Winter as joint Heads of its newly established Crisis Solutions class of business within its London Market Specialty division. Bringing them together is a defining moment for both the market and SiriusPoint, giving us the scale, expertise and credibility from day one to build a disciplined, durable portfolio.” David Govrin, Group President and Chief Executive Officer, Global Reinsurance & London Market Specialty, SiriusPoint, said, “The launch of Crisis Solutions and the hiring of Ed and Paul shortly after the establishment of our London Market Specialty division reflect our ambition to grow our position in the specialty classes where global clients have always sought the innovation, product expertise, and capacity of the London Market. The team will be based in London, with plans to build out underwriting, exposure management, and claims capabilities, supporting the Crisis Solutions business and SiriusPoint’s next phase of growth within the London Market Specialty division.

item.title: USI Insurance Services Claims Ex-Broker Poached Clients for Own New Agency
item.description: New York-based insurance broker USI Insurance Services has gone to court to halt a former employee it alleges has poached a few customers for his own newly-formed agency. According to USI, MacNair has used “USI’s confidential information and its reputation in the insurance industry to build the book of business he presided over on behalf of USI” and is now using that information and those relationships to build his new agency, USI is seeking monetary damages and an injunction to stop MacNair’s alleged “ongoing violations” of his agreements with USI. MacNair is violating employment and severance agreements by using confidential information and soliciting his former clients for his own Pennsylvania-based MacNair Enterprises LLC in direct competition with USI.

item.title: Supreme Court Tosses Florida Suit Over Immigrant Driver’s Licenses
item.description: The US Supreme Court refused to let Florida file an unusual lawsuit that accused California and Washington of making it too easy for undocumented immigrants to get commercial driver’s licenses. Related: California Revokes 17,000 Commercial Driver’s Licenses for Immigrants North Carolina May Lose $50M in Federal Funds Over Immigrant Trucker Licenses Giving no explanation, the justices on Tuesday rejected Florida’s bid to sue its fellow states directly in the Supreme Court, as states can do under some circumstances to protect their sovereign interests.

item.title: Viewpoint: Tackling the Threat of Legal System Abuse
item.description: The data strongly suggest the answer is legal system abuse – exploitative attorney advertising, litigation financing, and plaintiff attorney tactics that are generating litigation independently of underlying accident trends. Research conducted by the Insurance Information Institute and the Casualty Actuarial Society found that legal system abuse and related litigation trends have resulted in an estimated $231.6 billion to $281.2 billion increase in liability insurance losses over the past decade. Third-party litigation financing (TPLF) is when a funder unrelated to the case, such as a hedge fund, provides money to cover a plaintiff’s legal costs in exchange for a share of any damages awarded through judgment or settlement.

item.title: Addresscloud Adds European Wildfire Model
item.description: Addresscloud (Cambridge, England), a geocoding and property intelligence provider, has added a high-resolution pan-European wildfire risk model developed by Skyblu (Warsaw) to its platform. The move expands Addresscloud’s portfolio of wildfire risk data for insurers operating in Europe, with more than double the area burned in recent years compared with the early 2000s putting pressure on insurers to improve risk selection and pricing, according to the companies. Built using satellite observation data from 2001 to 2023 and enriched with climate and vegetation data, the model provides forward-looking wildfire probability insights for 2026 to 2030.

item.title: ‘Pronounced softening’ at Florida renewals bodes well for APAC insureds, but long-term risks are building
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: What Happens Next in Iran — and What It Means for Insurers
item.description: and Iran still had to agree on what to do about Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium, about reopening the Strait of Hormuz and about U.S.-led economic sanctions against Iran. and Iran was 95% complete, I was reminded of a truism among software developers in the 1990s: "The first 80% of a project takes 90% of the time, and the final 20% takes 90% of the time." Comments on social media noted that even if negotiators had made it through 95% of the issues on their checklists, that progress meant that the U.S. Having covered an international mess or two in my time, I think the situation in Iran has all the markings of a long-term impasse.

item.title: Aon expands global credit reinsurance hub strategy
item.description: Aon announced changes to its global credit reinsurance structure as the broker looks to strengthen connections between clients and reinsurance capital across major markets. As part of the strategy, Rupert Evans, Aon’s international head of credit and financial risks reinsurance, will relocate from London to New York to support clients and capacity providers across the U.S. The company’s global credit reinsurance operations are led by Ben Walker, global credit reinsurance leader, alongside Evans and Nick Ayres, chairman of global credit.

item.title: Arch Insurance names Imran Jalozie CIO
item.description: Arch Insurance Company appointed Imran Jalozie as chief information officer, formalizing a role he has held on an interim basis since May 2025. Arch Insurance President Brian First said Jalozie’s leadership will help “accelerate the responsible adoption of new technologies, like artificial intelligence” while improving collaboration across IT and operations. Jalozie joined Arch in 2022 as vice president of IT application development and will now lead enterprise IT infrastructure and delivery efforts, including platform modernization and large scale transformation initiatives.

item.title: Asurion launches NEXT event series
item.description: Asurion launched “Asurion NEXT,” a new bi-annual event series focused on showcasing the company’s technology platforms, AI capabilities, and connected device support strategy. At the inaugural event, CEO Guru Gowrappan outlined Asurion’s push beyond traditional protection plans toward integrated support experiences spanning device setup, repair, replacement, and ongoing assistance across the home technology ecosystem. At Asurion NEXT, we demonstrate how our technology works seamlessly so people can live without interruption.

item.title: Del core a los componentes: la banca acelera una transformación sin “big bang”, pero con negocios más enfocados
item.description: La banca latinoamericana avanza hacia un modelo de modernización progresiva basado en componentes, dejando atrás las grandes implementaciones y migraciones “big bang” para adoptar plataformas más flexibles, abiertas y conectadas con los ecosistemas financieros, sin interrumpir la continuidad del negocio. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca. Durante años, los proyectos de transformación bancaria estuvieron marcados por un modelo que obligaba a esperar meses, incluso años, hasta tener todo listo antes de ejecutar el cambio.

item.title: KatRisk Joins Verisk Model Exchange
item.description: Verisk (Jersey City, N.J.) has announced that KatRisk has entered into an agreement to join Verisk Model Exchange, expanding access to third-party catastrophe risk models within Verisk’s open catastrophe risk modeling ecosystem. Adding KatRisk models to Verisk Model Exchange is intended to expand access to specialized catastrophe risk insights, particularly for new geographic territories and perils where additional modeling perspectives can support clearer understanding of exposure and resilience. “Verisk Model Exchange will allow the market to assess KatRisk’s models alongside other independent approaches within a common analytical framework supporting informed comparison and stronger risk insight across the industry.” Models available through Verisk Model Exchange span global natural catastrophe perils, including wildfire, severe convective storm, tropical cyclone, storm surge, inland flood and earthquake.

item.title: Register: Risky Future AI Tools for Embedded Platforms ‘Demo Day’ on May 27
item.description: Insurance Journal’s Risky Future series is hosting the “ AI Tools for Embedded Insurance Platforms ” Demo Day, a series of free AI tool demonstrations designed exclusively for (re)insurers, MGAs/Program Administrators, marketplaces, and other tech and distribution platforms. In partnership with Insurance Journal, Risky Future’s AI Tools for Homeowners Data Demo Day will be held on Wednesday, May 13. About the Risky Future “Demo Day” Events Risky Future Demo Day events are the latest in a series of live online events in which experts share solution demos addressing the latest challenges facing the insurance industry.

item.title: Hyundai Recalling 421,000 US Vehicles Over Software Brake Issue
item.description: Hyundai Motor is recalling 421,078 vehicles in the U.S. over a software error that may cause an unexpected application of brakes, the U.S. The auto safety agency said that a software error in the front cameras of the vehicles may cause the forward collision avoidance system to prematurely activate and cause the application of brakes, increasing the risk of a crash.

item.title: Milder than Normal Atlantic Hurricane Season Expected Thanks to El Niño
item.description: A developing El Niño that is forecast to get quite strong will likely dampen the upcoming Atlantic hurricane season, but it won’t make the potentially deadly storms disappear, federal and outside meteorologists predict. A normal hurricane season has 14 named storms, seven of them becoming hurricanes and three of them reaching major hurricane level, which is more than 110 mph (177 kph). This is after nine of the last 10 Atlantic hurricane seasons have been above normal or even hyperactive, Klotzbach said.

item.title: Florida Insurer Safepoint Eyes $1.16B Valuation in U.S. IPO
item.description: Insurer Safepoint said on Tuesday it was targeting a valuation of up to $1.16 billion in its U.S. Founded in 2013, Safepoint is a property and casualty insurer focused on delivering insurance in coastal markets such as Florida and Louisiana. initial public offering, riding a wave of post-reform Florida insurance growth.

item.title: East Coast Regional Workers Comp Insurer Reports Data Breach
item.description: The workers’ comp insurer recommends that those affected in the breach remain vigilant by regularly reviewing their credit reports and financial account statements for any unauthorized activity. The Beacon Mutual Insurance Company (Beacon Mutual), a regional workers’ compensation insurer serving Rhode Island, Massachusetts, and Connecticut, announced that it had completed an investigation surrounding a data security incident. Upon learning of the unauthorized activity on its computer network on January 14, 2026, the insurer took action to contain it and reported the incident to law enforcement.

item.title: ILS valuation now a ‘core determinant’ of market credibility, Smyth, Nascent Group
item.description: In recent years, the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market has expanded from a niche property catastrophe tool into a broader and more complex asset class, and with that evolution, valuation has also moved from a reporting exercise into a core determinant of market credibility, according to Andy Smyth of Nascent Group. With that evolution, valuation has moved from a reporting exercise to a core determinant of market credibility,” Smyth explains in a recent report. To conclude, Smyth observes that valuation encompasses both governance and technical aspects, highlighting how investors anticipate independence, transparency, and a clear expression of uncertainty, all of which are vital for maintaining market credibility.

item.title: KatRisk to join Verisk Model Exchange to broaden catastrophe risk modelling access
item.description: Verisk, a strategic data analytics and technology partner to the global insurance industry, has announced that KatRisk has agreed to join the Verisk Model Exchange as Verisk continues to develop its open catastrophe risk modelling ecosystem. “Building resilience in the face of growing catastrophe risk requires transparency, choice, and the ability to understand uncertainty,” added James Lay, assistant vice president of Verisk Model Exchange, Verisk Catastrophe and Risk Solutions. Verisk said the move is aimed at increasing access to third-party catastrophe risk insights for insurers and reinsurers, as climate-related hazards and changing regulatory requirements continue to raise expectations for clear and supportable risk assessment.

item.title: Property cat pricing trending down mid-teens at mid-year renewals: BMO Capital Markets
item.description: Property catastrophe reinsurance pricing is trending down mid-teens for the mid-year 2026 renewals, meeting or beating market expectations, according to BMO Capital Markets, who also expect prices to stay under pressure absent a significant $100 billion or more of reinsurance losses. Pricing remains the focus and pressure point of 6/1 renewals. While pricing has fallen, reinsurers have remained disciplined and reports suggest that attachment points are holding.

item.title: Record-Setting Drought Devastates Wheat Crop in Kansas
item.description: Orville Williams has had a healthy wheat crop on his 2,600-acre farm in Montezuma, Kansas, every year since he was a teenager. Record-setting drought and hotter-than-average temperatures mixed with sharp drops have impacted much of the U.S. Drought conditions have worsened the spread of the wheat streak mosaic virus and barley yellow dwarf virus, which impact the potential of the crop.

item.title: Inszone Acquires Michigan’s Legacy Partners
item.description: Inszone Insurance Services announced the acquisition of Michigan-based Legacy Partners Insurance Services. Founded in 2013 by Joe Graham, Legacy Partners has spent the last decade growing exponentially throughout Southeast Michigan. Operating out of its Harrison Township headquarters, the agency built its reputation on comprehensive service and a close-knit, highly experienced team Following the acquisition, the Legacy Partners team will maintain its strong roots in Southeast Michigan, continuing to operate primarily from its Harrison Township hub.

item.title: JPMorgan Banker Sues Ex-Colleague Over ‘Fabricated’ Sex Claims
item.description: banker named in a graphic sexual harassment suit that went viral last month hit back at her accuser with a defamation counterclaim, calling his allegations “entirely false” and “malicious.” Lorna Hajdini last Tuesday night countersued a former colleague on the bank’s leveraged finance team who claimed that she forced him to be her “sex slave” and used racist language to demean him. She “categorically and unequivocally” denied his “disgusting” claims, saying they have “ruined her reputation and destroyed her life.” The April 27 suit against JPMorgan and Hajdini was filed by a John Doe plaintiff, but people familiar with the matter have said he was Chirayu Rana, a former vice president at the bank. Hajdini and members of her family have been mocked, ridiculed, and harassed around the clock,” lawyers for the banker said, “with Ms.

item.title: State Farm reports Q1 2026 results for P&C companies
item.description: State Farm released the Q1 2026 financial results for 13 of its P&C carriers. State Farm’s auto carrier saw the biggest improvement, going from a $3.6 billion loss in Q1 2025 to a $1 billion gain in Q1 2026. During the quarter, the group of companies reported a net underwriting gain of $1.8 billion, compared to a $5.1 billion loss in Q1 2025.

item.title: Vertafore earns fourth consecutive Excellence in Customer Service Award
item.description: Vertafore , the leader in insurance technology, earned the 2026 Excellence in Customer Service Award from the Business Intelligence Group for the fourth consecutive year, reinforcing its leadership in customer satisfaction. That level of care and accessibility gives me confidence in the platform and reinforces our decision to grow with Vertafore.” The Excellence in Customer Service Award recognizes organizations that are transforming how companies engage with customers. Vertafore differentiates itself by prioritizing meaningful, high-quality interactions over traditional volume-driven support models, while empowering teams with deep industry expertise and AI-powered tools to deliver fast, accurate customer support.

item.title: CloudCover appointed Neil Danilowicz as Senior Principal Architect.
item.description: The post CloudCover appointed Neil Danilowicz as Senior Principal Architect. At CloudCover , Danilowicz will help advance the company’s C3C platform, which combines security controls, cyber risk monitoring, and insurance capabilities. Danilowicz joins the Minneapolis-based cybersecurity and cyber insurance company with more than 30 years of experience in networking and security.

item.title: Reinsurance Turning Point: From Operational Constraint to Competitive Advantage
item.description: Here, they examine why modernizing reinsurance has become a strategic imperative and what is required to achieve its full benefits. Yet amid this transformation, most insurers have largely left one critical function behind: reinsurance. Many organizations still rely on spreadsheets, manual workflows and isolated processes for reinsurance.

item.title: South Korea’s FTC raids 8 non-life insurers over suspected bid-rigging: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: In Charts: Seed Deals Keep Getting Bigger As Odds Of Reaching Series A Fall Dramatically
item.description: But while companies that are funded at the seed stage are typically raising larger checks, they’re also taking longer to move on to Series A and face lower odds of graduating to that phase at all, Crunchbase data shows. The economics of seed investing have changed dramatically since the AI boom began, a review of Crunchbase data shows. Seed rounds are larger than ever, with some startups now raising $8 million to $10 million deals once associated with later stages.

item.title: The IPO Comeback Has A Catch
item.description: But here’s the catch: those aren’t IPOs for the rest of the market. Every year for the past several years, the same prediction circulates: This is the year the IPO market comes back. And every year, a handful of headline-grabbing offerings get held up as proof.

item.title: 56% of planned US data centres are in catastrophe exposed locations: MS Amlin
item.description: A new analysis from Lloyd’s global specialist re/insurer MS Amlin has revealed that 56% of the 670 planned US data centre projects representing nearly $800 billion in investment, are located in states that are highly exposed to either hurricanes, severe convective storms, earthquakes or winterstorms. In addition, MS Amlin also noted that 27% of data centres, representing $440 billion in investment, are planned in states at high risk of winter storms, which can disrupt power networks and create complex business interruption risks. MS Amlin also observed that nearly a quarter (21%) of planned data centres, amounting to $340 billion of investment, are located in states at high risk of hurricanes, while data centres in high-risk earthquake states account for 3% of planned facilities, representing roughly $12 billion in investment.

item.title: RenRe saw stronger reinsurance demand ahead of mid-year renewals, CUO Marra
item.description: RenaissanceRe Group Chief Underwriting Officer (CUO) David Marra highlighted during the firm’s Q1 2026 earnings call, that new demand for reinsurance protection ahead of the key mid-year renewals was trending higher than the company initially projected at the January 1st renewals. The shift from public to private markets benefits the entire distribution chain, including increasing demand for reinsurance.” Marra also noted that RenRe grew in Florida through both its acquisition of Validus and organically in 2025. During the firm’s earnings call, Marra outlined that the Bermuda-based reinsurer and third-party capital management specialist is making good progress on the US mid-year renewals.

item.title: Galapagos Capital SSPE issues largest LRS at R$126m, the fourth under Brazil’s ILS regime
item.description: Gálapagos Capital Sociedade Seguradora de Propósito Específico S.A., the Brazil based SSPE or special purpose reinsurance vehicle that can issue Letra de Risco de Seguro (Insurance Risk Letters) and is owned by global investment company Galapagos Capital, has completed issuance of the largest transaction under Brazil’s insurance-linked securities regime so far. Gálapagos Capital SSPE has issued a R$126 million offering of LRS which is the first broadly distributed LRS arrangement since Brazil put its own ILS regulations into law and activity commenced in the country. The model represents an important shift in the traditional market logic by establishing a direct link between those who take risks and those seeking investment opportunities.” Roberto Takatsu, partner in the insurance business at Galapagos Capital, highlighted how this larger and more broadly distributed offering demonstrates Brazil’s ILS market activity gaining pace, “The accelerated dissemination of this instrument in the market implies a long learning period, which is only now gaining more traction.

item.title: Generali unveils Redion brand for global Care platform
item.description: Global insurer Generali has revealed Redion, the new brand for its global Care platform, bringing together the activities of Europ Assistance and Generali Employee Benefits (GEB) under a single identity and offering. Giulio Terzariol, Group Deputy CEO of Generali, said, “Redion is the expression of what Generali Care has already become: a global, integrated platform, purpose-built to deliver comprehensive Care across every dimension of people’s lives. Fully aligned with our “Lifetime Partner 27: Driving Excellence” strategy and our ambition to lead in protection, health and accident, Redion embodies a simple, immediate and consistent standard of Care, bringing together complementary capabilities in prevention, insurance and assistance in one seamless, global proposition.” Jean-Laurent Granier, CEO of Generali France & Global Business Activities and Chairman of Redion, added, “I sit in three seats at this table — as Chairman of Redion, as a network partner through GEB, and as a client on the assistance side.

item.title: Frontline gets new Florida reciprocal insurer FIRE rated by KBRA
item.description: Credit rating agency KBRA has assigned a BBB+ Insurance Financial Strength Rating (IFSR) to Frontline Insurance Reciprocal Exchange (FIRE), a newly formed Florida-domiciled reciprocal insurer. A key driver of the positive rating is FIRE’s alignment with Frontline Insurance Group’s (Frontline) established Florida residential property platform, largely through First Protective Insurance Company (FPIC). FIRE was established to write admitted Florida residential property business, focusing primarily on homeowners and fire/dwelling coverage.

item.title: Christian Hoffman named Aon’s global CEO of Commercial Risk
item.description: Global re/insurance broker and risk advisor Aon has announced the appointment of Christian Hoffman as global CEO of Commercial Risk, effective June 1. Hoffman will leverage his 25 years of experience in the insurance and financial services industries as both a broker and underwriter to lead Aon’s global Commercial Risk business. His deep specialty experience and understanding of how to connect risk with the right forms of capital position him exceptionally well to lead Commercial Risk at a time when clients need greater clarity, flexibility and innovation.” Hoffman currently serves as Aon’s Global Specialty & Financial Products Leader and Head of M&A for North America.

item.title: First Brands Hit by $286 Million Claim for Alleged Tariffs Fraud
item.description: The US government has joined the line of creditors impacted by alleged fraud at bankrupt autoparts maker First Brands, in this instance for accusations the company cheated on how much it should have paid on tariffs. First Brands was hit with a formal claim for $285.5 million related to allegations that it underpaid levies on parts imported from China. That lawsuit came years before First Brands collapsed into bankruptcy and lenders accused the company of widespread fraud.

item.title: California Narrows Evacuation Area With Risk ‘Off the Table’
item.description: There is no longer a risk of a compromised chemical tank exploding in the suburbs of Los Angeles and evacuation orders for some of the more than 50,000 people were lifted, according to the Orange County Fire Authority. On Monday, the Orange County Fire Authority interim chief said the threat of explosion was now “off the table” after firefighters found and verified a crack that relieved pressure in the tank. As the crisis stretches on, schools in the affected area have rescheduled graduations and told parents to prepare for online learning on Tuesday.

item.title: US Supreme Court Denies Meta’s Challenge to Vermont Social Media Addiction Lawsuit
item.description: Supreme Court declined on Tuesday to hear a bid by Meta Platforms to avoid a lawsuit brought by Vermont’s attorney general accusing the company of designing its Instagram social media app to be addictive to young users, as big technology companies face mounting legal risks over child and teen safety. The justices turned away Meta’s appeal of a lower court’s ruling that let the lawsuit proceed, rejecting the company’s argument that courts in Vermont lack jurisdiction over the dispute. Testifying in February at a youth social media addiction trial in California, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg denied that Instagram targets kids.

item.title: GM National Insurance Company releases Q1 2026 results
item.description: GM National Insurance Company, the licensed carrier that’s part of General Motors, published its Q1 2026 results, ending the period with $29.8 million in written premiums, up from $9.5 million in Q1 2025. The company posted a net underwriting loss of $23.4 million in Q1 2026, compared to a $15.4 million loss in Q1 2025. Loss & LAE ratio improved by about 23 points YoY.
